ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn does not have a specific entry for Chicken Tandoori Pide. The closest parent is chicken pide as a general Turkish flatbread with chicken topping. Tandoori spice variant is not separately coded.
ontology.foodex2_description: Pide (Turkish flatbread) with meat filling — closest parent category in FoodEx2 composite dish hierarchy
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for Chicken Tandoori Pide. A0FKP (pide with meat) is the nearest defensible parent. Tandoori spice profile is not separately coded in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT clinical concept exists for Chicken Tandoori Pide or Turkish pide as a food entity.

main_ingredients: pide dough (wheat flour, yeast, water, salt)
tandoori spice blend (cumin, coriander, paprika, turmeric, chilli, garam masala)

serving.natural_language_portion: One whole Chicken Tandoori Pide, approximately 350 g, as typically served at a Turkish restaurant in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Chicken Pide — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy composite estimate; supplemented with USDA Foundation Foods data for tandoori-marinated chicken (NDB No. 05064 roasted chicken breast, spiced) and pide dough analogue (NDB No. 18033 pita bread, white)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct entry for Chicken Tandoori Pide exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, IMR Malaysia MyFCD, or FAO/INFOODS SEA databases. Values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy components: roasted spiced chicken breast (~55% by weight) and white pita/flatbread dough (~45% by weight), with adjustment for tandoori marinade (yogurt, spices, oil). Energy and macros are weighted composites. Sodium is elevated to reflect spice blend and marinade salt. Values represent a plausible per-100g nutritional profile for this dish category.

gi_source.title: Pita bread, white wheat — Glycemic Index Database; supplemented with Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC (2008) International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values. Diabetes Care 31(12):2281–2283
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Chicken Tandoori Pide. GI is estimated from the dominant carbohydrate vehicle: white pita/flatbread, which has a published GI of approximately 57 (medium) per the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 international tables. The chicken and spice components contribute negligible glycemic load. GL per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (57 × 82.25) / 100 ≈ 46.9, where carbohydrate per 350 g serving = 23.5 × 3.5 = 82.25 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~480 mg per 100 g; ~1680 mg per full serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to half a pide or less, as a full serving provides approximately 1680 mg sodium — approaching the daily recommended limit of 2000 mg for hypertensive adults — and should request reduced salt in the marinade and dough where possible.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium glycemic index (GI 57) and high glycemic load per full serving (~46.9 GL units)
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume a half-portion (approximately 175 g) to limit glycemic load to around 23 GL units, pair the pide with a non-starchy vegetable side to slow glucose absorption, and monitor postprandial blood glucose given the substantial refined wheat flour content of the pide dough.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate saturated fat (~1.6 g per 100 g) and elevated sodium (~480 mg per 100 g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For individuals with cardiovascular disease, Chicken Tandoori Pide is preferable to red-meat pide variants due to the lean chicken protein, but the sodium content and refined carbohydrate load warrant portion control; a half-serving with a salad is a more heart-appropriate meal configuration.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Chicken Tandoori Pide (also rendered as Chicken Tandoori Pidde) is a Turkish-origin baked flatbread dish in which a boat-shaped pide dough — made from wheat flour, yeast, water, and salt — is topped with tandoori-marinated chicken pieces and baked in a high-heat oven until the crust is golden and slightly charred at the edges. The tandoori marinade typically consists of yogurt, cumin, coriander, paprika, turmeric, chilli powder, garam masala, garlic, and lemon juice, imparting a vivid reddish-orange colour and a smoky, mildly spiced flavour profile to the chicken. The dish fuses Turkish pide tradition with South Asian tandoori cooking technique, a combination that has become popular in Turkish-Lebanese restaurants in Singapore, including Sultan Palace at Boat Quay. Nutritionally, the dish provides a moderate energy density of approximately 218 kcal per 100 g, with a meaningful protein contribution (~15.8 g per 100 g) from the chicken, and a carbohydrate load (~23.5 g per 100 g) primarily from the wheat dough. The medium glycemic index (~57) and high glycemic load per full serving make portion control important for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or metabolic syndrome. Sodium content is elevated (~480 mg per 100 g) due to the spice blend and dough salt, which is a consideration for hypertensive individuals. The dish is halal-suitable when prepared with halal-certified chicken and is not suitable for vegetarians, vegans, or those with gluten or dairy allergies.

confidence_reason: No direct entry for Chicken Tandoori Pide exists in any approved primary regional database (HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, IMR Malaysia MyFCD, FAO/INFOODS SEA). Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy component data for roasted spiced chicken breast and white pita flatbread, weighted by approximate ingredient proportions. GI is estimated from the analogous white pita flatbread GI published in the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are component-weighted estimates derived from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (roasted spiced chicken breast NDB 05064 and white pita bread NDB 18033) in approximately 55:45 chicken-to-dough ratio by weight, with adjustment for marinade ingredients. No direct analytical measurement of this dish exists in any approved primary source. 2. vitamin_d_mcg is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy entries used for this composite estimate and is not reliably measurable without direct food analysis. 3. GI of 57 is an analogous estimate based on white pita flatbread as the dominant glycemic carbohydrate vehicle; the actual GI of this specific dish has not been directly measured. 4. The dish name 'Pidde' is a transliteration variant of the Turkish word 'Pide' (flatbread); both spellings are used in Singapore restaurant menus. 5. Halal status is flagged as true based on the restaurant context (Sultan Palace Turkish and Lebanese Cuisine, Boat Quay, Singapore), which operates as a halal establishment; this should be verified against current MUIS certification. 6. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are estimated from USDA component data and should be treated as indicative rather than analytically confirmed values.
